PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 5 Sub-batch 5.1d Area B SHIPPED (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out via BullMQ — main commit 068d32f). Continues [[feedback_marine_guardian_opus_executor_default]] doctrine in spirit but flags a dispatch-mechanism risk: main-session fallback is safe and worked here, but the pattern of "subagent returns interim status with agentId hint, no progress" needs root-cause investigation before next dispatch. LAST_DONE:    Sub-batch 5.1d Area B (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as 068d32f. 2 files changed, +241 / -4. Modified: apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/areaBoundary.ts (+57 LOC — `fanOutAreaRederive(tenantId, userId): Promise<{enqueued: number}>` private helper; queries Event + Patrol + FuelEntry via Promise.all with explicit `where: { tenantId }` defense-in-depth scoping per security.md; enqueueAreaRederive per row with entity casing "event"|"patrol"|"fuelEntry" matching AreaDerivationEntity contract from 5.1b helper; userId threaded from ctx.userId since adminProcedure guarantees it and BaseJobPayload requires it; wired into create unconditionally, into update + delete gated on `result.count > 0` to avoid spurious fan-outs on no-op writes; return shape extended from raw Prisma result to `{boundary|result, fanOut}`); apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/areaBoundary.test.ts (+188 LOC — extended prisma mock with event/patrol/fuelEntry findMany; mocked @marine-guardian/jobs for enqueueAreaRederive; updated existing create test assertion from `result.id` → `result.boundary.id` per new return shape; +6 new test cases in dedicated describe block: (1) create fans out for every Event+Patrol+FuelEntry with correct payload shape per entity, (2) create fan-out queries scoped to requesting tenant with explicit tenantId where clauses, (3) update fans out when result.count > 0, (4) update does NOT fan out when result.count === 0 (no findMany calls either), (5) delete fans out when result.count > 0, (6) delete does NOT fan out when result.count === 0). Initial typecheck flagged that BaseJobPayload requires `userId` (interface line 3) — fixed by threading ctx.userId through fanOutAreaRederive signature and updating all 5 enqueueAreaRederive calls; tests updated to assert userId in payload. Verification (both on feat/5.1d-area-boundary-cud-fanout branch AND independently re-verified on main after squash-merge): web lint 0/0 errors+warnings, web typecheck 0 errors, web tests 274/274 PASS (was 268 baseline + 6 net new = matches expected delta), jobs lint+typecheck clean, jobs tests 57/57 unchanged (5.1d-B is web-only). Total monorepo test count 325 → 331 (+6). Branch feat/5.1d-area-boundary-cud-fanout single commit bbd1bee → squash-merged to main as 068d32f → branch force-deleted per Rule 23 standard cleanup. NO AuditLog write — deferred to 5.1e per Option A scope split (admin manual-rebuild has full ctx.session.userId context). NO jobs package changes — 5.1d-B is consumer-side wiring only; the queue + processor + worker built in 5.1c handle the producer/consumer flow unchanged. Scope adjustment from original brief: Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change — v2 L546) deferred. Original brief assumed sync upserts compare incoming vs persisted areaName and call applyAreaDerivation on change, but ErEvent/ErPatrol interfaces in packages/jobs/src/lib/earthranger-client.ts do not currently include area_name, AND the syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads in er-sync.processor.ts do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" — so Area A's precondition is an unbuilt sync-engine feature (area_name ingestion). Implementing Area A would require either bootstrapping that ingestion (out of scope for 5.1d as scoped) or implementing a phantom feature that never fires because sync never sees areaName changes. User confirmed Ship Area B Only path via AskUserQuestion. Dispatch path experience for this sub-batch: 2 consecutive Opus 4.7 subagent dispatches (ab538b0b5337b5cae, abbccff0dd9aba1b8) returned what reads as interim status messages mid-stream ("Reading the queue and area-rederive worker to verify enqueueAreaRederive signature, and verifying jobs barrel exports:" followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint; "Noted — skill suggestion ignored per [[feedback_skill_injection_hooks_ignore_when_locked_stack]]. Tech stack is locked in inputs.yml (priority 6) which overrides skill suggestions (priority 7). Continuing." followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint). Both burned ~70s + ~10 tool uses each (~287K + ~264K total_tokens reported) with zero git state changes — verified via git log/status/branch. SendMessage tool not surfaced in inventory (ToolSearch "select:SendMessage" returned "No matching deferred tools found" twice), so neither agent could be continued. User chose main-session Opus 4.7 implementation as the unblock; entire 5.1d-B work landed cleanly in ~10 minutes including governance. The dispatch-mechanism issue is the only open question — could be (a) framework-level Agent output budget that returned interim narration as terminal output, (b) PreToolUse hook noise (skill injection) that the executor interpreted as a stop signal, or (c) some Opus 4.7 subagent behavior where the model returns at natural sentence boundaries when uncertain. Worth investigating before next dispatch — but main-session is a safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches under ~30K main-session tokens.

Modified: packages/jobs/src/queues/types.ts (+AreaRederiveJobPayload + QUEUE_NAMES.AREA_REDERIVE='area-rederive'), queues/index.ts (+5 LOC exports), processors/index.ts (+1 LOC), workers/index.ts (+5 LOC), workers/base-worker.ts (+9 LOC — optional `limiter?: RateLimiterOptions` WorkerOption + conditional spread guarded by `options?.limiter !== undefined`, backward-compatible so existing 4 workers (er-sync/alerts/email/maintenance) unaffected), start-workers.ts (+5 LOC — startAreaRederiveWorker registered as 5th worker), packages/jobs/src/index.ts (+17 LOC barrel re-exports incl. applyAreaDerivation for 5.1e admin tRPC consumption), packages/jobs/package.json (+@marine-guardian/shared workspace:* dep alongside existing @marine-guardian/db, +`./lib/area-derivation` subpath export entry), pnpm-lock.yaml (+3 lines for new workspace dep edge). Reason: 5.1d sync engine processor lives in @marine-guardian/jobs and apps→packages dependency direction is one-way (apps depend on packages, packages NEVER depend on apps); 5.1e admin tRPC import surface becomes `import { applyAreaDerivation } from "@marine-guardian/jobs"` via root barrel re-export. Dispatch experience: single Opus 4.7 executor (via Agent(model: "opus")) reported STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with 6 reasonable concerns: (1) helper relocation apps/web→packages/jobs architecturally required not optional — 5.1d processor in jobs needs helper, apps→packages is one-way; (2) added @marine-guardian/shared workspace dep — required by relocated helper's deriveArea import; (3) `platformPrisma as unknown as ExtendedPrismaClient` cast with explanatory comment — type-narrowing decision documented inline so 5.1d processor (next sub-batch) inherits the pattern; (4) base-worker.ts extended with optional limiter field, backward-compatible; (5) two commits collapsed via squash-merge as standard; (6) AREA_REDERIVE_CONCURRENCY=10 chosen as conservative default — v2 L545 specifies limiter not concurrency, reasoning: limiter is binding constraint (50 jobs/sec ceiling) so concurrency just prevents Redis fetch latency bottleneck. Architect's call on which path. Either way, Area A is unblocked once area_name flows through sync.
